name: premarket_gappers_scanner
description: Declarative spec for a premarket gappers scan. This file is configuration/documentation only; it does not execute on its own. Run it with an LLM agent that has a WebFetch-style tool (e.g. `claude -p "run the scan defined in premarket_gappers.json" --allowedTools WebFetch`).

parse_fields.gap_pct: percent change / gap percent as a number (e.g. 7.5, not "7.5%")
parse_fields.premarket_volume: volume as an integer; expand abbreviations (1.2M -> 1200000, 850K -> 850000, 2.3B -> 2300000000)

data_source.notes: Yahoo's gainers table exposes regular-session volume, not a true premarket figure; premarket_volume is populated from that column.

news_catalyst.prompt_template: What recent news or catalyst is driving {TICKER} stock today? Return a one-sentence summary, then up to 2 recent headlines verbatim. Just the data — no commentary.

do_not_use.reason: returns HTTP 503 reliably; Benzinga is the working source

on_failure.behavior: set catalyst=null and headlines=[] for that ticker; do not abort the whole scan
on_failure.applies_when: fetch fails OR no ticker-specific catalyst is found (generic/unrelated market headlines do not count)

gappers.headlines: array of up to 2 verbatim strings (empty array if none)

example_row.catalyst: Beat Q1 earnings, raised FY guidance

summary_line.format: Premarket Gappers: N names. Top: TICKER1 (X%) — catalyst1, TICKER2 (Y%) — catalyst2, TICKER3 (Z%) — catalyst3
summary_line.when: printed after the output file is saved
